content.problemStatement:
Read problems statements in Russian and Bengali.Chef has an array AAA with NNN elements. He wants to find NNN points P1,‚Ä¶,PNP_1, \dots, P_NP1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äč with integer coordinates on the 2D coordinate plane such that, for all pairs of indices iii and jjj (1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N), the Manhattan distance from PiP_iPi‚Äč to PjP_jPj‚Äč is Ai+AjA_i+A_jAi‚Äč+Aj‚Äč. Help him find any NNN points satisfying the condition, or state that no such points exist.

As a reminder, the **Manhattan distance** between the points (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1‚Äč,y1‚Äč) and (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2‚Äč,y2‚Äč) is defined as ‚ą£x1‚ąíx2‚ą£+‚ą£y1‚ąíy2‚ą£|x_1 - x_2| + |y_1 - y_2|‚ą£x1‚Äč‚ąíx2‚Äč‚ą£+‚ą£y1‚Äč‚ąíy2‚Äč‚ą£.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The TTT test cases then follow.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, if there is no set of points satisfying the stated condition, output `NO`.


Otherwise, output `YES` on one line, then output NNN lines. Each line should contain two integers XiX_iXi‚Äč and YiY_iYi‚Äč (‚ąí1018‚Č§Xi,Yi‚Č§1018-10^{18} \leq X_i, Y_i \leq 10^{18}‚ąí1018‚Č§Xi‚Äč,Yi‚Äč‚Č§1018) denoting the xxx and yyy coordinates of the point PiP_iPi‚Äč respectively.


It can be proven that if a solution exists, then a solution exists under the constraints above.


Output is case insensitive.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first test case, we can choose P1=(3,9)P_1 = (3,9)P1‚Äč=(3,9) and P2=(13,17)P_2 = (13,17)P2‚Äč=(13,17). The Manhattan distance between P1P_1P1‚Äč and P2P_2P2‚Äč is ‚ą£3‚ąí13‚ą£+‚ą£9‚ąí17‚ą£=18|3-13|+|9-17|=18‚ą£3‚ąí13‚ą£+‚ą£9‚ąí17‚ą£=18, and A1+A2=5+13=18A_1 + A_2 = 5 + 13 = 18A1‚Äč+A2‚Äč=5+13=18.


For the second test case, we can choose P1=(18,5)P_1 = (18,5)P1‚Äč=(18,5), P2=(10,1)P_2 = (10,1)P2‚Äč=(10,1), and P3=(1,16)P_3 = (1,16)P3‚Äč=(1,16).

The Manhattan distance between P1P_1P1‚Äč and P2P_2P2‚Äč is ‚ą£18‚ąí10‚ą£+‚ą£5‚ąí1‚ą£=12|18-10|+|5-1|=12‚ą£18‚ąí10‚ą£+‚ą£5‚ąí1‚ą£=12, and A1+A2=8+4=12A_1 + A_2 = 8 + 4 = 12A1‚Äč+A2‚Äč=8+4=12.
The Manhattan distance between P1P_1P1‚Äč and P3P_3P3‚Äč is ‚ą£18‚ąí1‚ą£+‚ą£5‚ąí16‚ą£=28|18-1|+|5-16|=28‚ą£18‚ąí1‚ą£+‚ą£5‚ąí16‚ą£=28, and A1+A3=8+20=28A_1 + A_3 = 8 + 20 = 28A1‚Äč+A3‚Äč=8+20=28.
The Manhattan distance between P2P_2P2‚Äč and P3P_3P3‚Äč is ‚ą£10‚ąí1‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí16‚ą£=24|10-1|+|1-16|=24‚ą£10‚ąí1‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí16‚ą£=24, and A2+A3=4+20=24A_2 + A_3 = 4 + 20 = 24A2‚Äč+A3‚Äč=4+20=24.



For the third test case, it can be proven that there does not exist 555 points satisfying these constraints.
